what_it_does: Renders the selected-node inspector panel: node identity, served status tinted by a registry token, a derived freshness verdict, editable generic config via NodeConfigForm, an output well, and actions to force rerun, open workshop, surface to inbox, and build from output.
observations: Uses kit components SectionHead, Badge, Surface, and EmptyState.
Status badge color is driven by nodeStates[selected.status].render.token via an inline style object.
Freshness badge is derived, address-guarded, and fail-loud ('unknown — <reason>').
NodeConfigForm is keyed with selected.nodeId + configTick to force remount after config writes.
Output actions only render when selected.output is truthy.
Preserves data-ui-ref attributes for inspector, freshness, act, workshop, surface, and build.
Inline style object is typed as any.
Comments are dense with external rule references (L10, F3, U1, etc.).
standouts: Status color comes from the served registry token rather than a hardcoded ternary.
Freshness is shown as a separate derived verdict alongside the served cached status.
The generic NodeConfigForm is intentionally left untouched as the designed config surface.
Output has a compact action footer linking to workshop, inbox rerun, and grow/build flows.
conventions: Functional React component consuming context via useApp.
Uses a design-system kit vocabulary (SectionHead, Badge, Surface).
Fail-loud: failed/stuck reasons and freshness unknown are surfaced explicitly.
Inline style used for dynamic theming when a kit prop cannot accept a CSS variable token.
concerns: statusStyle is typed as any, weakening type safety.
CSS variable name is interpolated into style strings without validation.
configByNode.current is read directly in render from a mutable ref.
The status badge is a custom span rather than the kit Badge component used elsewhere.
Heavy comment annotations reference external rules and may drift out of sync.
notes: A right-rail inspector that reads node state from the served registry and augments it with a derived freshness verdict, keeping the generic config form untouched.
questions: What is the exact shape of nodeStates registry entries and the render.token contract?
How and when is freshness fetched, and does address equality alone guarantee the verdict is current?
Why is configTick used to remount NodeConfigForm instead of reactive prop updates?
What are the expected arguments and side effects of surfaceOutput and buildFromOutput?
Are selected.error and selected.status guaranteed to be synchronized?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to render a kit-styled selected-node inspector while preserving the generic NodeConfigForm and data-ui-refs; the implementation matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Recognition by sight via kit components
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make the inspector a concise command-bridge surface where operators can see status, freshness, config, and output actions at a glance without reimplementing the generic config editor.
novelty: The freshness verdict is rendered as a separate, address-guarded derived badge alongside the served status, and status color is sourced from the registry token rather than local logic.
contribution: A focused selected-node detail panel that combines served status, derived freshness, generic config editing, and output actions, plus the pattern of registry-driven status coloring and guarded on-demand freshness derivation.
summary_for_embedding: Inspector.tsx is a React region component that renders the selected node's identity, registry-tinted status, derived freshness, generic NodeConfigForm, output well, and output actions in a right-rail panel.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to redesign the selected-node inspector to a kit-based command-bridge vocabulary while preserving the existing generic config surface and telemetry refs.

symbols.does: Renders the selected-node inspector panel with status, freshness, config, output, and actions.
symbols.description: Inspector is a functional React component that consumes AppContext to display the currently selected node. It renders the node type header, kind and status badges (colored by the served registry token), a derived freshness badge, failure/stuck reasons, node metadata, a generic NodeConfigForm for editing config, an output Surface, and conditional actions to force rerun, open workshop, surface to inbox, and build from output.
